name: the primitive type called ooze

phase_0.boundary_condition: The universal coupling field of the interaction grammar

VINIT.justification: The state of isolated computational units before any interaction grammar is applied.

TANCH.justification: The boundary of the ooze primitive which contains all participating nodes in a single broadcast domain.

AFWD.justification: The forward movement of data from a source into the universal coupling medium.

AREV.justification: The return signal or reflection from the field boundary back to the source.

CLINK.justification: The sequential attachment of individual nodes to the universal broadcast bus.

IMSCRIB.justification: A node recognizing its own broadcast within the universal field.

FSPLIT.justification: A single signal splitting into parallel paths across the all-to-all network.

FFUSE.justification: The reconstitution of the original signal from the collective state of the network.

EVALT.justification: Successful synchronization across the broadcast domain.

EVALF.justification: Failure to maintain universal coupling due to signal interference.

ENGAGR.justification: A node simultaneously acting as both a source and a sink in a non-resolved state.

IFIX.justification: The permanent record of a universal broadcast event in the system history.

phase_3.void_description: uninitialized network state with zero coupling
phase_3.both_description: entangled state where nodes are both coupled and independent

establish the node identity within the field

process the back-propagation from the secondary branch
detect interference or loss on the secondary branch
hold the state of partial coherence across the network
reconstitute the universal signal from the matrix
verify the integrity of the fused signal
seal the broadcast domain within the terminal anchor

phase_5.memory_mechanism: Distributed field state across all coupled nodes
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The causal ordering of the interaction grammar

phase_6.cycle_cost: Signal propagation energy across the coupling field
